content:
Moodle Unizulu Faculty of Science — Complete Access Guide

The University of Zululand (Unizulu) Faculty of Science uses Moodle as its primary online Learning Management System (LMS). Whether you're studying Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Mathematics, or Computer Science, all your Unizulu science course materials, assignments, and online tests are hosted on the Unizulu LMS education platform at lms.unizulu.ac.za.

Access Moodle — Unizulu Faculty of Science

🔗 Go to Unizulu Moodle → lms.unizulu.ac.za

Log in with your:
- Unizulu student number (your username)
- Unizulu network password

After logging in, scroll to "My Courses" to see all your Faculty of Science modules.

Unizulu Faculty of Science — Departments on Moodle

The Faculty of Science at Unizulu offers undergraduate and postgraduate programmes across several departments. All are supported on the Unizulu Moodle LMS platform:

DepartmentExample Moodle ModulesLevel
Biochemistry & MicrobiologyMolecular Biology, Microbiology Practicals, Biochemistry 2BSc
BotanyPlant Ecology, Systematics, EthnobotanyBSc / BSc Honours
ChemistryOrganic Chemistry 1, Physical Chemistry, Analytical ChemistryBSc
Computer Science & ITProgramming 101, Data Structures, Networking FundamentalsBSc / BTech
Mathematics & Applied MathematicsCalculus, Linear Algebra, Statistics 2BSc
PhysicsClassical Mechanics, Electromagnetism, Quantum PhysicsBSc
ZoologyAnimal Diversity, Ecology, Conservation BiologyBSc / BSc Honours

How to Find Your Science Module on Unizulu Moodle

  1. Log in to lms.unizulu.ac.za with your Unizulu student number and password.
  2. Click "Dashboard" in the top navigation bar.
  3. Look under "My Courses" — all your registered Faculty of Science modules will appear here.
  4. If a module is missing: Check with your department administrator or contact your lecturer directly. Modules are visible only after the lecturer activates them on Moodle.
  5. Search for a module: Use the search bar at the top of the Moodle page. Type the module code (e.g., "CHEM201") or the module name to find it.

Unizulu Faculty of Science — Moodle Features for Students

Submitting Assignments on Unizulu Moodle

Most Faculty of Science assignments at Unizulu are submitted via Moodle:

  • Open your module and click on the assignment name
  • Click "Add submission" and upload your PDF or document file
  • Click "Save changes" before the deadline
  • You will receive a confirmation notification to your Unizulu student email

Online Science Practicals and Tests

Some Unizulu Faculty of Science assessments — particularly in Chemistry, Physics, and Computer Science — are conducted as online quizzes on Moodle. These may be time-limited. Ensure you have a stable internet connection and are using a compatible browser (Chrome or Firefox).

Lecture Notes and Resources

Lecturers in the Faculty of Science upload notes, recorded lectures, textbook chapters, and supplementary reading to Moodle. Check your course page at least twice a week for new content.

Unizulu Moodle Support — Faculty of Science Contacts

ContactDetails
Unizulu ICT Helpdesk+27 35 902 6000 | ictservices@unizulu.ac.za
Faculty of Science Office+27 35 902 6802 (KwaDlangezwa campus)
Moodle Technical SupportEmail your faculty administrator with the module name and error description

2026 Bursary Assistance for Unizulu Faculty of Science Students

Science students at the University of Zululand are among the most eligible for funded bursaries in South Africa. The country urgently needs qualified scientists, engineers, and IT professionals — and bursary funders know it.

StudyBuddy helps Unizulu Faculty of Science students find and apply for bursaries in 2026:

  • 🔬 Science & Research bursaries — National Research Foundation (NRF), Medical Research Council (MRC), and Department of Science & Innovation fund Unizulu science students
  • 💻 ICT & Computer Science bursaries — MICT SETA, MTN, Vodacom, and government ICT bursaries for Computer Science and IT students
  • ⚗️ Chemistry & Environmental Science — Sasol, Transnet, and Richards Bay Minerals (RBM) fund chemistry and environmental science graduates from KZN universities
  • 🌿 Agricultural & Biological Sciences — Department of Agriculture, DAFF, and Sappi fund biology, ecology, and zoology students in KwaZulu-Natal
  • 📐 Mathematics & Statistics — Actuarial and data science bursaries are available for strong Maths students at Unizulu
  • ⏰ 2026 application windows open now — Most science bursaries close between March and June 2026

👉 Find 2026 bursaries for Unizulu science students →

👉 Get 24/7 AI academic support for science subjects →

Also see: Unizulu Moodle Login & ITS Guide →

⚠️ Disclaimer: StudyBuddy is an independent student support platform and is not officially affiliated with the University of Zululand (Unizulu), the Faculty of Science, or Moodle. All Unizulu trademarks belong to their respective owners. Your data is protected under POPIA and is only used to facilitate bursary assistance.

faqs.question: How do I access Moodle for the Unizulu Faculty of Science?
faqs.answer: Go to lms.unizulu.ac.za and log in with your University of Zululand student number and password. After logging in, find your Faculty of Science modules under 'My Courses' on your Moodle dashboard.

faqs.question: Why can't I see my Faculty of Science module on Unizulu Moodle?
faqs.answer: Modules appear on Unizulu Moodle only after your lecturer activates them. Allow 24–48 hours after the start of the semester. If a module is still missing, contact your department administrator in the Faculty of Science or email ictservices@unizulu.ac.za.

faqs.question: What science departments are supported by Unizulu Moodle?
faqs.answer: All departments in the Unizulu Faculty of Science use Moodle LMS, including Biochemistry & Microbiology, Botany, Chemistry, Computer Science & IT, Mathematics, Physics, and Zoology.

faqs.question: How do I submit a science assignment on Unizulu Moodle?
faqs.answer: Open your module on lms.unizulu.ac.za, click the assignment name, click 'Add submission', upload your file, and click 'Save changes'. Ensure you submit before the deadline — late submissions may not be accepted.

faqs.question: Are there bursaries for Unizulu Faculty of Science students in 2026?
faqs.answer: Yes. Unizulu science students can apply for NRF bursaries, ICT sector bursaries (MICT SETA, Vodacom, MTN), chemistry and environmental bursaries from Sasol and Richards Bay Minerals, and KwaZulu-Natal provincial science funding. StudyBuddy helps students identify and apply before 2026 deadlines.
